summary:
The goal is to improve tube function through surgical intervention before considering assisted reproductive technologies due to established bilateral tubal blocks; thus, laparoscopic/hysterectomy approach should precede any further steps involving fertility treatment such as IVF directly without prior attempt at restoration. Agent2 reasoned that addressing the underlying cause—tubal obstructions—with procedures aimed at correcting them offers potential benefits over immediately opting for alternative methods like IVF which does not address these specific issues. Thus, beginning with techniques capable of assessing and treating those conditions makes sense clinically based on available data about this particular case scenario. \boxed{Laparoscopy and hysteroscopy are recommended initially}. Summary:

The objective is to correct tubal blockage via laparoscopy and hysteroscopy before proceeding to more advanced fertility treatments since the patient has confirmed bilateral tubal blockages from an earlier examination. This targeted procedure aims to restore tube function naturally rather than relying solely on artificial insemination methods right away. \boxed{\text{Ilektric}}
